title: Funding: The Compensation for Employers of Reservists Program – Develop your application - Canada.ca
content:
## Funding: The Compensation for Employers of Reservists Program – Develop your application
- 2. Eligibility information
- 3. Assessment process
- 4. Develop your application

## 4. Develop your application
It is recommended that you consult this section while filling in the Grant Application Package , as it provides important details on the information and supporting documentation ESDC requires to assess your application. Provide your answers on the Grant Application Form and attach only the documents requested (consult the Apply page).
All required documents must be attached, otherwise your application will be considered incomplete. You are required to submit your application in one complete package once the reservist has returned from active military activity (including any pre-deployment training deployment and post-deployment activities that are mandatory, including leave).

### How to fill out your Grant Application Package
The Grant Application Package consists of the following sections:
Section A – Program information
This section provides a description of Compensation for Employers of Reservists Program (CERP) including all relevant information and support for the applicant.
Section B – Notice to applicants
This section provides the applicant with information about the collection of personal information.
Section C – Grant application for funding under CERP:
- Part 1 – Organization
- Part 2 – Reservist, mission or training, and employment information
- Part 3 – Annex (for Quebec applicants only)
Section D1 – Articles of agreement
This section provides all contract information between Canada and the applicant organization once the submitted Grant Application Package is approved by the Department of National Defence.
Section D2 – Declarations and signatures
This section provides a list of declarations that the applicant must certify to prior to submitting their Grant Application Package.
Section D3 – Canada approval
This section is to be completed by Canada.

### Section C – Grant application for funding under CERP

#### Part 1 – Organization
ESDC uses the information you provide in this section to establish your organization's identity.
Question 1 – Organization legal name
Please provide the legal name of your organization. Usually, this is the name associated with your registration with the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA), or the name that would appear on funding cheques.
Question 2 – Operating (Common) name (if different from legal name)
Please provide the operating (or common) name of your organization if it is different from the legal name.
Question 3 – Business number
Please provide your 15-digit CRA business number (for registered charities and not-for-profit organizations, your registration number). You must provide a document that includes your business or registration number (e.g. tax related documents, CRA correspondence, etc.).
Example: 123456789 RR 0001
For more information, please visit the CRA website .
Question 4 – Organization type
Your answer to this question must be one of the following:
- Employer of a reservist
- Self-employed reservist
- Refer to Eligibility
Question 5 – Year established
Please indicate the year your organization was established.
Question 6 – Organization address
Please indicate the address of your organization – Applicants must provide a complete address in a format recognized by Canada Post. Information is available on the Canada Post website .
Questions 7 and 8 – Telephone and fax
Please indicate the telephone and fax numbers of the organization.
Question 9 – Email address
Please indicate the email address of the organization.
Question 10 – Mailing address
Please indicate the mailing address if different from your organization's address.
Questions 11 and 12 – Telephone and fax
Please indicate the telephone and fax number if different from your organization's address.
Please provide a main contact for the purposes of communicating with your organization about this grant. This could be the business owner/manager or a delegate of their choice.
Questions 13 and 14 – Name
Please provide the name of the person in your organization who will be Employment and Social Development Canada's (ESDC) main contact for the grant.
Question 15 – Position title
Please provide the title of the organization contact identified in questions 13 and 14.
Example: Business owner, President, Executive Director.
Question 16 – Preferred language of communication
Please indicate the preferred language of the organization's main contact for both written and spoken communication.
Question 17 – Organization contact – Address
Please indicate whether the address for the Organization Contact is the same address as the Organization Address or the Organization Mailing Address given in Section 1A. If different, please indicate.
Questions 17.1 to 17.4 – Contact address, telephone, fax and email address
If you selected "Different" in Question 17, please provide the address, the telephone and fax numbers and the e-mail address for the Organization Contact here.
Question 18 – Does your organization owe any amounts to the Government of Canada?
If yes, please indicate the amounts owing in the spaces provided.
A2. Nature of amount owing
A3. Department or agency to which money is owed
Employment and Social Development Canada
Question A4 – Is a payment plan in place?
Please indicate whether or not payment arrangements have been made.
Answering "yes" to this question will not invalidate your application. However, you must provide details as to the type of debt owed and the arrangements that have been made for repayment.

#### Part 2 – Reservist, mission or training, and employment information
ESDC uses this information to determine if the reservist, the mission or training and the employment situation meet all of the program eligibility requirements.
Question 19 – Given name(s)
Please provide the given name of the reservist.
Question 20 – Surname
Please provide the surname of the reservist.
Question 21 – Military service number
Please provide the reservist's military service number.
Question 22.1 – Mission or training name
Please provide the name of the Chief of Defence Staff directed operation for which the reservist is taking a leave of absence from work.
Question 22.2 – Start date of military activity
Please provide the date the reservist began his/her active military activity.
Question 22.3 – End date of military activity
Please provide the date the reservist was released from active military activity. This represents the date when the reservist was deemed to have completed all the requirements of his/her mission or training. In order for your organization to be eligible for compensation under this program, the leave of absence for active military activity must have a minimum duration of 30 days .
Question 24 – Date the reservist started working for your organization
Please provide the date the reservist started working for your organization. In order for your organization to be eligible to receive compensation under this program, the reservist must have worked for your organization for a minimum of 13 consecutive weeks (3 consecutive months) prior to the leave of absence for active military activity.
Question 25 – Average number of hours per week the reservist works for your organization
Please provide the average number of hours per week the reservist works for your organization. You must provide copies of the reservist's pay stubs for the 13 consecutive weeks (3 consecutive months) prior to the leave of absence for active military activity with your completed Grant Application Package. The information contained in the pay-stubs submitted with your application will be used to confirm the average number of hours indicated under this question.
In order for your organization to be eligible to receive compensation under this program, the reservist must have worked an average of 30 hours or more per week for your organization over 13 consecutive weeks (3 consecutive months) prior to the leave of absence for active military activity.
Self-employed reservist – Your business currently represents and it has represented your main source of employment/income for at least 13 consecutive weeks (3 consecutive months) prior to the leave of absence for active military activity.
In order to be eligible to receive compensation under this program, self-employed reservists must demonstrate that the self-employment has represented their main source of employment/income for a period of at least 13 consecutive weeks (3 consecutive months) prior to the leave of absence for active military activity. You must provide a copy of your most recent financial statements signed by a certified accountant with your completed Grant Application Package (which includes the 13 consecutive weeks (3 consecutive months) prior to the leave of absence for active military activity).
Please ensure to include all the additional required documents with your Grant Application Package.
Please indicate how did you find out about the CERP program. If Other, please indicate the answer in the space provided.
In the first  'Choose File' box, please upload a Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) document that includes your CRA business number.
In the second 'Choose File' box, please upload copies of the reservist's pay stubs for the 13 consecutive weeks.
If additional copies of the reservist's pay stubs need to be uploaded, please proceed to Supporting Documents p.7B. These fields are optional and may be omitted if not required.

#### Part 3 – Annex (for Quebec applicants only)
To be completed by organizations located in Quebec: Please note sections 3.11 and 3.12 of the Act respecting the ministère du Conseil exécutif (R.S.Q.c. M-30) and ensure to answer questions 1 and 2 of this part in the Grant Application Package.
Once completed and submitted, this CERP Grant Application Package, if approved by Employment and Social Development Canada, will form the agreement between Canada and the organization. The organization will then be subject to the Articles of Agreement included in the Grant Application Package.
Please ensure that your application is submitted by an official, authorized representative of your organization. People with signing authority are normally one or more of the executive members of the board of directors (President, Vice President, Secretary or Treasurer) and employees of the organization (Chief Executive Officer, Executive Director, Chiefs of Finance or Human Resources).
The Grant Application Package must be completed in accordance with the organization's statutes, by-laws or other constituting documents. For example, the president and the chief financial officer may be required to sign all outgoing documents.
Please review and confirm all entered information and make any necessary changes before selecting 'Submit application'.
You should not assume any commitment on the part of Department of National Defence (DND) or ESDC (referred to as «Canada») until funding has been approved and the Grant Application Package has been signed by a representative of Canada. ESDC will notify you in writing of the outcome of the review of your application.
Notice to applicants
The information you provide in this Grant Application Package is collected under the authority of the Department of Employment and Social Development Act for the purpose of assessing eligibility of your application.
Participation in CERP is voluntary. If you do not provide all of the personal information required in this application, your application will not be processed.
As part of the assessment process and issuance of the grant, information may be shared with the Department of National Defence, Public Services and Procurement, and Members of Parliament.
The information you provide may also be used and/or disclosed for policy analysis, research, and/or evaluation purposes. However, these additional uses and/or disclosures of your personal information will never result in an administrative decision being made about your application.
Your personal information is administered in accordance with the Department of Employment and Social Development Act, the Privacy Act and other applicable laws. You have the right to the protection of, and access to, your personal information, which is described in Personal Information Bank ESDC PPU 721. Instructions for obtaining this information are outlined in the government publication entitled Info Source . Info Source may also be accessed on-line at any Service Canada Centre.

- Employers supporting Reservists
- Access to Information and Privacy

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
